answer: repositories, branches, commits, and pull requests
A README file is a file with information about your project.
The changes on your branch will be incorporated into the main branch.

In the upper-right corner of any page, select , then click New repository.

In the "Repository name" box, type hello-world.

In the "Description" box, type a short description. For example, type "This repository is for practicing the GitHub Flow."

Select whether your repository will be Public or Private.

Select Add a README file.

Click Create repository.
